title: Voice Nodes
description: Enable the voice processing pipeline, configure a wake word, test speech-to-text transcription, and test text-to-speech response generation.

steps.instruction: Enable the voice processing module in the configuration.
steps.action: The configuration is updated with `voice.enabled: true`, `voice.stt.engine: "whisper"`, `voice.tts.engine: "openai"`, and `voice.inputFormat: "wav"`.
steps.expectedOutput: `openclaw config show voice` prints the enabled voice config with STT and TTS engines. The Gateway log shows `Voice module initialized (STT: whisper, TTS: openai)`.

steps.instruction: Configure a custom wake word that activates voice mode.
steps.action: The terminal runs `openclaw voice wake-word --set "hey clawby" --sensitivity 0.8` which trains a lightweight wake-word detector on the provided phrase.
steps.expectedOutput: `Wake word set: "hey clawby" (sensitivity: 0.8)`. A test utterance of the wake word triggers `Wake word detected!` in the logs.

steps.instruction: Test the speech-to-text pipeline by sending an audio file for transcription.
steps.action: The terminal runs `openclaw voice stt --input test-audio.wav` which sends the WAV file through the Whisper model and returns the transcribed text.
steps.expectedOutput: The transcription is printed: `"What is the weather like today?"` with a confidence score of `0.94`. Processing time is displayed.

steps.instruction: Test the text-to-speech pipeline by generating an audio response from an agent reply.
steps.action: The user sends a text message. The agent replies, and the TTS engine converts the response to audio. The terminal runs `openclaw voice tts --text "The weather is sunny and 22 degrees." --output reply.mp3`.
steps.expectedOutput: An `reply.mp3` file is generated. The terminal prints `TTS generated: reply.mp3 (2.1s, 32KB)`. Playing the file produces a natural-sounding voice reading the response.

completionMessage: Congratulations! The voice pipeline is fully operational. You can speak to your agent, have it transcribe your words, process them, and respond with synthesized speech.
